entities.name: GENETIC ENGINEERING
entities.description: The process of introducing selected genetic regions with desirable traits into target plants for spatial and temporal expressions.

entities.name: PROMOTERS
entities.description: Key genetic elements responsible for regulating gene expressions by modulating transcription factors through recognition of RNA polymerases.

entities.name: GENE EXPRESSION
entities.description: The process of synthesizing functional gene products, regulated by promoters and transcription factors.

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTION FACTORS
entities.description: Proteins that are modulated by promoters to regulate gene expression.

entities.name: RNA POLYMERASES
entities.description: Enzymes that recognize promoters to initiate the transcription process.

entities.name: RNA POL II
entities.description: A category of RNA polymerase based on its recognition and expression patterns.

entities.name: RNA POL III PROMOTERS
entities.description: Promoters recognized by RNA polymerase III, offering controlled regulation with minimum adverse effects.

entities.name: TRANSGENE EXPRESSION
entities.description: The expression of a transgene in a plant, regulated by promoter activity and specificity.

entities.name: CONSTITUTIVE PROMOTERS
entities.description: Promoters that are active across all tissues and stages, which may cause adverse effects on non-target organisms.

entities.name: CAULIFLOWER MOSAIC VIRUS 35S
entities.description: A specific example of a constitutive promoter used in plant genetic engineering.

entities.name: NONTARGET ORGANISMS
entities.description: Organisms other than the target plant that may be adversely affected by the use of constitutive promoters.

entities.name: ECOSYSTEM
entities.description: The biological environment that may be adversely affected by the use of constitutive promoters.

entities.name: TISSUE SPECIFIC PROMOTERS
entities.description: Inducible or tissue-specific promoters that provide controlled gene expression with minimum adverse effects.

entities.name: SYNTHETIC BIOLOGY
entities.description: A field of science influenced by the role of promoters in transgene expression.

entities.name: GENOME EDITING
entities.description: A method of genetic modification influenced by the role of promoters in transgene expression.

entities.name: TRANSGENIC CROPS
entities.description: Crops developed through genetic engineering to be nutritionally and agronomically improved for commercialization.
